skill.name: slack-gif-creator
skill.description: Creates animated GIFs optimized for Slack emoji and messages using Pillow, ImageIO, validation helpers, and animation guidance.
skill.summary: Create and validate polished Slack-ready animated GIFs.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Official Anthropic skill reviewed with a minimal audit. The skill contains local Python helpers for image generation, GIF optimization, and validation, with no prompt injection attempts or suspicious network behavior found.

content.value_statement: Slack GIFs often fail because dimensions, timing, colors, or file size are wrong. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code create, optimize, and validate animated GIFs for Slack.
actual_capabilities: Creates animated GIFs from generated Pillow frames or uploaded images.
Optimizes emoji GIFs to 128 by 128 pixels with reduced colors.
Builds message GIFs with recommended square dimensions for Slack sharing.
Applies easing, bounce, pulse, slide, fade, zoom, spin, and particle concepts.
Validates GIF dimensions, frame count, duration, file size, and frame rate.
Provides helper functions for gradients, circles, stars, text, and frame composition.
limitations: It does not include a library of premade characters, icons, or brand assets.
It relies on installed Python image libraries such as Pillow, ImageIO, and NumPy.
It cannot guarantee Slack acceptance if workspace policy or Slack limits change.
Text rendering uses default fonts unless the user adds custom font handling.

use_cases.title: Team Emoji Creation
use_cases.description: Create compact animated emoji for team reactions, launches, celebrations, and recurring rituals.

use_cases.title: Product Update Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate short branded message GIFs that make product announcements easier to notice in Slack.

use_cases.title: Design Prototype Motion
use_cases.description: Test simple motion ideas such as bounce, pulse, and slide before producing final assets.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Emoji GIF
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a Slack emoji.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 128 by 128 Slack emoji GIF of a bright star pulsing twice, then settling. Keep it under three seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Uploaded Image Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my uploaded image as the main subject. Make a Slack-ready GIF where it gently bounces and sparkles, then validate the output.

prompt_templates.title: Message GIF With Motion
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a larger GIF for a Slack channel post.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 480 by 480 Slack message GIF for a product launch. Use a slide-in title, confetti burst, and smooth easing.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize And Compare
prompt_templates.prompt: Create two versions of the Slack GIF: one high quality and one smaller emoji-optimized version. Compare file size, frame count, duration, and validation results.

output_examples.input: Make a Slack emoji GIF of a rocket taking off.
output_examples.output: A 128 by 128 animated rocket emoji with smoke puffs, upward motion, reduced colors, and validation results for Slack emoji dimensions.

output_examples.input: Turn this uploaded logo into a celebration GIF for Slack.
output_examples.output: A short square GIF using the logo as the center object, with pulsing scale, sparkles, a clean loop, and a file size summary.

output_examples.input: Make a product launch announcement GIF for a channel message.
output_examples.output: A 480 by 480 message GIF with slide-in text, accent shapes, confetti motion, and practical optimization notes.

best_practices: Use 128 by 128 pixels, 10 to 15 frames per second, and short loops for emoji GIFs.
Validate every final GIF before sharing it in Slack.
Use fewer colors and frames only when file size needs reduction.
anti_patterns: Do not depend on emoji fonts or unavailable premade graphics.
Do not create long, high-frame-rate emoji loops that become large files.
Do not skip validation when resizing, optimizing, or using uploaded images.

faq.question: What size should Slack emoji GIFs use?
faq.answer: Use 128 by 128 pixels when possible. Square GIFs from 64 to 128 pixels can also be acceptable.

faq.question: Can this skill create GIFs from uploaded images?
faq.answer: Yes. It can load uploaded images with Pillow and use them directly or as visual references.

faq.question: Does it include premade Slack emoji assets?
faq.answer: No. It provides utilities and guidance, but it does not include a built-in asset library.

faq.question: How does it reduce GIF file size?
faq.answer: It can reduce dimensions, frame count, color count, and duplicate frames during GIF export.

faq.question: Can it validate existing GIFs?
faq.answer: Yes. The validator checks dimensions, file size, frame count, duration, and frame rate.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
